method: LLM selector (from union of A+B) + Quant allocator (MIQP-equivalent, LAMBDA=2.0)

per_pick_rationale.rationale: Dominant custom-silicon and networking partner to hyperscalers with a $73B AI backlog and best-in-class ~45% operating margins; trades at a forward earnings discount to peers, creating a compelling execution-driven compounding story.

Unmatched near-term AI infrastructure execution with the fastest Blackwell ramp in history and ~75% gross margins; fortress balance sheet and CUDA moat provide downside defense despite forward demand debates.

Mission-critical enterprise workflow platform with $27.7B in remaining performance obligations and 97% renewal rates; AI pivot toward non-seat pricing and orchestration offers a path to reaccelerate cRPO growth.

Entrenched leader in AI data-center Ethernet switching with $6.2B in deferred revenue and software-like ~42% operating margins; supply-constrained premium is offset by prohibitive customer switching costs.

Global sleep-apnea leader trading at a GLP-1 fear discount despite accelerating mask resupply and a locked-out primary competitor; best-in-class free cash flow conversion and durable pricing power support a rerating.

Fast-growing IBSRELA franchise with formulation protection to 2042 and recent refinancing that pushed maturities to 2030; profitability inflection and working-capital normalization could drive significant upside asymmetry.

Center of the largest pharma profit-pool expansion in history with tirzepatide volume surging 40–50% and Medicare obesity coverage opening in mid-2026; vertical integration protects best-in-class margins despite premium valuation.

Hyper-dominant ANZ employment marketplace with 4.9x placement share trading at distressed valuations; Platform Unification yield expansion and an anticipated Employment Hero divestment offer balance-sheet de-risking and rerating potential.

Global streaming utility generating ~32% operating margins and $12.5B free cash flow at a forward earnings discount to peers; scaling ad tier and aggressive buybacks provide a mechanical valuation floor.

Core advertising monopoly reaching 3.56B daily users with AI-augmented pricing power and segment margins near 50%; stock discounts peak-capex fears, offering re-rating as operating income proves resilient above $83B.

Hybrid defensive-yield and digital-infrastructure compounder with 46% Data FFO growth and inflation-linked legacy cash flows; discounted entry and ~5% distribution yield create rare income-plus-growth asymmetry.
Contracted nuclear and retail base providing near-term EBITDA visibility; scarce, NRC-licensed assets offer direct exposure to AI-driven power scarcity, balanced by active FERC/PJM regulatory risks.

Imminent Coterra merger close with a pre-committed 31% dividend increase and >$5B buyback authorization; pro forma breakeven below $50 WTI and $1B synergy target create a catalyst-rich, discounted E&P setup.

Payment-network duopoly with resilient cross-border recovery and mid-teens Value-Added Services growth; aggressive share repurchases and a modest valuation discount to Visa support steady earnings compounding.

World's largest mining truck-body fabricator trading below tangible book after self-inflicted operational wounds; new CEO, Chile contract exit, and North American labor fixes offer a discrete, catalyst-driven recovery path.

llm_parsed.comparison_notes: The union of Track A and Track B comprises exactly 15 unique tickers, indicating that the quant optimizer and the LLM analyst fully converged on the same underlying names. Track A’s MILP approach likely emphasized the probability-weighted expected returns, correlation-aware diversification, and sector feasibility, while Track B expressed qualitative conviction through active position sizing and detailed fundamental rationales. I side with this consensus: every candidate satisfies the hard feasibility constraints, carries a positive base return, and contributes a distinct risk/reward profile. The portfolio intentionally balances high-factor-similarity technology exposure (AVGO, NVDA, ANET, NOW) with defensive healthcare compounders (LLY, RMD, ARDX), cash-generative communication services (META, NFLX, SEK.AX), yield-plus-growth infrastructure (BIP, VST), a capital-return energy catalyst (DVN), a quality financial compounder (MA), and a deep-value industrial event bet (ANG.AX). By retaining all 15, the final list preserves both the quant engine’s return maximization and the LLM’s thematic diversification.
llm_parsed.portfolio_thesis: The consolidated portfolio is a high-conviction, 15-name barbell that pairs durable cash-flow compounders in healthcare and communication services with event-driven inflections in energy, utilities, and industrials, while maintaining core AI-technology exposure. The blend offers sector and geographic diversification, multiple valuation backstops, and a mix of defensive yield, operational execution, and asymmetric turnaround narratives designed to compound through a range of macro outcomes.
key_risks: Technology cluster factor similarity (AVGO, NVDA, ANET, NOW) creates correlated drawdown risk if AI capital expenditure cycles disappoint or credit conditions tighten sharply.
Regulatory and legislative headwinds could simultaneously impair VST (FERC/PJM rulemaking), BIP (complexity discount and rate exposure), and MA (interchange fee caps).
Healthcare valuation sensitivity means GLP-1 substitution fears or reimbursement shocks could pressure LLY, RMD, and ARDX concurrently.
ANG.AX remains a binary turnaround where governance or operational execution failure could produce severe impairment.
Commodity price volatility and leveraged balance sheets in DVN, BIP, and VST amplify downside if energy prices or interest rates shift abruptly.
